title: Doctor Prime Treatment ABI (Parts VI and XIII)
law: complete_bits / original_weight_count <= 1/1, exact Fraction, via tools/foundry/one_bit_ceiling.py
what_it_is: An exact byte layout for every Doctor treatment: header(192 bits fixed) | params block | declarative fields | pad to byte. One generic bit codec over a per-treatment layout function; payload values are unsigned ints of declared width, bf16 carried as raw uint16 so round-trip is integer-exact. Decode is self-describing (params are serialized).

checks.round_trip: decode(encode(t)) == t asserted for all 7 non-wrapper treatments plus the wrapper
checks.exactness: independent closed-form bit count for sparse_residual equals total_bits() equals len(encode())*8 exactly
checks.conditional: installed_bits > inner total_bits; active_bits_per_token = installed * fire_rate; assert installed >= active
checks.no_dense_shadow: requires_dense_shadow() true when native-tagged elements >= rows*cols; ledger_components refuses it
checks.ledger: ten one_bit_ceiling components emitted; component sum == serialized bit sum; run through assert_complete_bpw_le_one
checks.ceiling_bites: same treatment set against 1024 weights raises CeilingViolation
checks.fails_when_broken: codebook field width flipped 16->15 makes the self-check fail (True)

honesty: This module counts bits. It measures no capability, no error, no recovery. An exact layout says nothing about whether a treatment helps. Demo payloads are random bits, not fitted parameters, and the demo weight count (rows*cols*4096) is a fixture, not a parent.
